overview: 本轮执行全量 MyCR：初始 open PR 36 个，最终 open PR 31 个；处理 12 个外部候选，其中 [#2030](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2030)、[#1991](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/1991)、[#2000](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2000)、[#2025](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2025)、[#2021](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2021) 通过 xhigh 子审和主线程复核后提交精确 `LGTM` 并用 `--match-head-commit` squash merge；[#2017](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2017)、[#2027](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2027)、[#1980](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/1980)、[#2029](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2029)、[#2022](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2022)、[#2026](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2026)、[#2018](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2018) 收到共 10 条 inline review comment。最终没有剩余“main base、非 draft、CI 全绿、无线程/无本轮 finding”的可合入外部 PR；唯一表面 CI 绿且 mergeable 的 [#1980](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/1980) 已有本轮两个有效 blocker。

采集 36 个 open PR；bulk GraphQL 504 后切换为 bounded per-PR collector；确认 locked PR 为 #1400/#1183。
planner 因旧 run_state 缺新 fingerprint 字段将 36 个 PR 全部标记 heavy_review；随后用 readiness gate 缩小真实候选集。
审批并合入 #2030；目标测试 `go test . -run TestSQLDBClient_Transaction -count=1` 通过。
陆续对 #2017/#2027/#1980/#2029/#2022/#2026/#2018 提交 inline review comments，并逐条回读 GitHub discussion URL。
审批并合入 #1991/#2000/#2025/#2021；每个 PR 合入前均刷新 head/checks，并用 match-head squash merge。

approved.title: fix: add panic recovery to MySQL Transaction to prevent tx leak
approved.author: shanghai-Jerry
approved.modules: storage/mysql transaction wrapper and sqlmock transaction tests
approved.technical_background: `storage/mysql` exposes a small SQL client abstraction used by storage/session modules. Its `Transaction` helper opens a `database/sql` transaction, passes `*sql.Tx` to caller code, and must either commit exactly once or roll back when user code fails. A panic is different from a returned error: without a defer/recover guard, the panic unwinds past the helper and leaves the transaction open until the driver notices the abandoned connection.
approved.problem: The PR fixes a transaction-leak gap when the callback panics. The old implementation rolled back only when the callback returned an error, so panic paths could leave an open transaction after partially executed statements.
approved.problem_framing: The immediate symptom is leaked MySQL transactions. The root cause is that callback-style transaction helpers must treat panic as another abnormal exit path and centralize rollback in a defer rather than only around explicit `error` returns.
approved.root_cause: Rollback lived next to `fn(tx)` error handling, not in a deferred cleanup block that observes every exit path.
approved.approach: The implementation changes the callback assignment from short declaration to the outer `err`, adds a deferred function that rolls back on panic or non-nil callback error, and re-panics after rollback. It keeps the commit path unchanged. Tests cover panic before and after an exec plus returned-error rollback.
approved.alternative_designs: A named return value plus defer could also express the same cleanup contract. A more invasive helper could wrap commit errors too, but that would change existing semantics unnecessarily.
approved.tradeoffs: The chosen design is narrow and low-risk: it adds rollback on panic without changing successful transactions or commit-failure behavior. It intentionally ignores rollback errors, consistent with the existing error path.
approved.design_assessment: Good local fix. It reaches the right balance for a storage helper: catch the leak, preserve panic semantics, avoid expanding the transaction API.
approved.change_inventory: Modified `sqlDBClient.Transaction`; added three `TestSQLDBClient_Transaction` subcases for panic rollback and normal error rollback.
approved.api_surface: No exported API change. The semantic contract of existing `Client.Transaction` becomes safer on panic.
approved.semantic_changes: A panic inside the transaction callback now rolls back before propagating. Returned errors still roll back; successful callbacks still commit.
approved.module_impact: Directly affects MySQL storage users. Indirectly protects callers that execute multiple statements in callback transactions.
approved.cross_module_impact: No cross-module contract changes beyond safer cleanup behavior.
approved.behavior_impact: Before: panic could leak an open transaction. After: panic triggers rollback and then propagates unchanged.
approved.tests_docs: `go test . -run TestSQLDBClient_Transaction -count=1` passed in an isolated worktree under `storage/mysql`; GitHub checks were green.
approved.attention_points: Commit failures still behave as before; this PR intentionally does not roll back after commit failure.
approved.outcome: xhigh 子审无发现；主线程复核 diff 和目标测试后提交 `LGTM`，并按 head `5fd16ff5bae4e2ea8229329c62eb589f24975ec7` squash merge。
approved.ci_state: Final refresh showed no failing or pending checks before merge.

approved.title: docs/design: remove debuglog plugin design doc
approved.author: Flash-LHR
approved.technical_background: `docs/design` stores design notes that are not necessarily part of the published MkDocs navigation. Removing an obsolete design-only file is mostly a repository hygiene change, but it still needs checking for navigation or inbound references.
approved.problem: The PR removes an obsolete DebugLog plugin design document that no longer represents the current direction of the repository.
approved.problem_framing: The underlying issue is stale design documentation: keeping unimplemented or superseded plans can mislead contributors about supported plugin APIs.
approved.root_cause: A historical design note remained in-tree after the corresponding direction was no longer current.
approved.approach: Deletes `docs/design/debuglog-plugin.md` without changing code or MkDocs navigation.
approved.alternative_designs: The file could have been rewritten as an archived rationale, but deletion is simpler when the design is not an active supported contract.
approved.tradeoffs: Deletion reduces stale guidance, at the cost of losing historical context in the docs tree. Git history still preserves it.
approved.design_assessment: Appropriate cleanup because no published navigation/reference dependency was found.
approved.api_surface: No exported API or user-visible runtime behavior change.
approved.behavior_impact: Readers no longer see the stale design note in source browsing.
approved.tests_docs: No tests needed for a standalone unreferenced design-doc deletion; GitHub checks were green.
approved.attention_points: If the team wants archived design history, use an explicit archive page later.
approved.outcome: xhigh 子审无发现；主线程确认无 navigation/reference risk 后提交 `LGTM`，并按 head `7f4c4813b847c840b0ae23ce93f55a3a7e9f53b3` squash merge。
approved.ci_state: Final refresh showed no failing or pending checks before merge.

approved.title: docs: clarify summary sync behavior
approved.author: Rememorio
approved.technical_background: Session summary has both async background workers and a synchronous intra-run refresh option. `WithAsyncSummaryNum` controls only async worker concurrency, while `llmagent.WithSyncSummaryIntraRun(true)` controls whether a long ReAct/tool loop refreshes summary before the next model call in the same `Run`.
approved.problem: The existing docs could be read as if async worker count selected sync vs async summary mode or disabled summaries, which is not the actual API model.
approved.problem_framing: The deeper problem is naming ambiguity: concurrency knobs and execution-mode knobs are separate, but both are near the summary setup path.
approved.root_cause: The docs lacked an explicit explanation tying `WithAsyncSummaryNum`, `WithSummarizer`, and `WithSyncSummaryIntraRun` to separate responsibilities.
approved.approach: Adds EN/ZH clarification that `WithAsyncSummaryNum` is not a mode switch, keeps the main example on async defaults, and documents the synchronous intra-run path plus its fallback/serialization caveats.
approved.alternative_designs: The docs could move this into a separate advanced section only, but putting a short warning near setup prevents the common misread earlier.
approved.tradeoffs: More text in the quick-start area, but it prevents misconfiguration of summary generation.
approved.design_assessment: Good documentation fix; it teaches the behavior without changing APIs.
approved.change_inventory: Updated English and Chinese session summary docs with mode and trigger timing clarifications.
approved.api_surface: No exported API change. Documents existing `llmagent.WithSyncSummaryIntraRun(true)` and summary options.
approved.module_impact: Documentation only; affects users configuring session summary.
approved.behavior_impact: Users are less likely to set worker concurrency expecting sync behavior or summary disablement.
approved.tests_docs: Docs-only PR with green GitHub checks.
approved.outcome: xhigh 子审无发现；主线程复核 docs 与实现语义一致后提交 `LGTM`，并按 head `f49f80253d2761bdf3f130793510dcb780a48003` squash merge。
approved.ci_state: Final refresh showed no failing or pending checks before merge.

approved.title: {evaluation, server/promptiter, examples}: add tool description optimization
approved.author: Flash-LHR
approved.technical_background: PromptIter optimizes prompts by running evaluation rounds, aggregating gradients, proposing patches, validating them, and accepting only improvements. Tool descriptions are part of the model-visible prompt: a weak tool `Description` can cause poor tool selection even when the Go function and schema are correct. A tool-description surface therefore lets the optimizer patch only the declaration text while preserving the actual tool ID, input schema, output schema, and implementation.
approved.problem: The PR adds first-class tool-description optimization so PromptIter can improve static tool declaration descriptions, not only broader prompt text.
approved.problem_framing: The root product need is prompt quality for tool-using agents. The submitted design treats tool descriptions as a targeted surface, which is narrower and safer than letting the optimizer rewrite full tool schemas.
approved.root_cause: PromptIter previously lacked a structured surface and sanitizer for changing only tool declaration text.
approved.approach: Adds tool-surface extraction and sanitization, optimizer structured output for `Description`/`Reason`, engine tests for targeted tool surface acceptance, server tests, EN/ZH docs, and a runnable travel lookup example that optimizes a misleading tool description.
approved.alternative_designs: A looser design could let the optimizer rewrite the whole tool declaration, but that would risk schema/function drift. Another design could keep this as example-only logic, but then server/workflow users would not get reusable support.
approved.tradeoffs: The chosen surface is conservative: it improves selection text while preserving schemas and execution code. It adds some surface/patch machinery, but tests show schema fields remain unchanged.
approved.design_assessment: Good fit for the root problem. It is a focused extension rather than an overbroad prompt rewrite path.
approved.change_inventory: Adds tool description docs, engine/profile/surface/optimizer changes, sanitizer tests, promptiter server tests, and `examples/evaluation/promptiter/tooldesc` data/code/README.
approved.api_surface: User-visible additions include tool-surface behavior in PromptIter docs/examples and structured optimizer output for tool-description patches. Existing schemas/functions are preserved.
approved.semantic_changes: PromptIter can now target tool declaration descriptions and accept/reject those patches by validation score. Tool execution still goes through original Go functions.
approved.module_impact: Direct impact on PromptIter workflow/server/example modules. Indirect impact on agent/tool users who rely on generated tool descriptions for model routing.
approved.cross_module_impact: Touches evaluation workflow, server handler, docs, and example modules but does not alter normal tool execution.
approved.behavior_impact: Before: tool-description quality had to be edited manually or via broad prompt changes. After: PromptIter can optimize a selected tool description and report accepted patches.
approved.tests_docs: GitHub checks were green. CodeRabbit suggestions were reviewed and judged non-blocking example/docs polish rather than merge blockers.
approved.attention_points: Future polish could assert printed example summary lines and clarify `-data-dir`; not required before merge.
approved.outcome: xhigh 子审无发现；主线程复核 bot suggestions 后提交 `LGTM`，并按 head `9cd9e9d33ead6773f77f3cdb30fbf5438749e154` squash merge。
approved.risk: Low to medium because the change is broad, but green CI and sanitizer tests cover the high-risk schema-preservation contract.
approved.ci_state: Final refresh showed no failing or pending checks before merge.

approved.title: build(deps): update vulnerable go dependencies
approved.author: Rememorio
approved.technical_background: This repository is a multi-module Go workspace. A dependency security update touches many module files because examples, storage drivers, model adapters, servers, and optional integrations pin their own `go.mod`/`go.sum`. The review risk is compatibility across shared stacks such as gRPC, protobuf, OpenTelemetry, `x/net`, `x/text`, and transitive security updates.
approved.problem: The PR updates vulnerable Go dependencies across modules to newer patched versions.
approved.problem_framing: The underlying issue is supply-chain hygiene. Because optional modules pin dependencies independently, vulnerability remediation must be repeated across many module boundaries instead of one root module.
approved.root_cause: Multiple standalone modules hold separate dependency graphs, so vulnerable transitive versions can remain even after root updates.
approved.approach: Updates go.mod/go.sum files across root, examples, model/storage/memory/knowledge/server modules; keeps code unchanged. CI validates build/test/lint across discovered modules.
approved.alternative_designs: A smaller PR could update only vulnerable modules, but broad alignment reduces repeated security drift. A workspace-level dependency policy would reduce future churn but is outside this PR.
approved.tradeoffs: Broad dependency updates increase review surface and potential version skew, but the lack of code changes and 162 green checks reduce immediate risk.
approved.design_assessment: Acceptable for a security/dependency maintenance PR. The important gate is green module CI and no obvious incompatible major-version jump in the reviewed stacks.
approved.change_inventory: Updated dependency versions and sums in 119 files, including `golang.org/x/net`, `x/text`, `grpc`, `protobuf`, OpenTelemetry, and module-specific transitive dependencies.
approved.api_surface: No exported project API change. The externally visible contract is dependency version selection for consumers of individual modules.
approved.semantic_changes: Runtime behavior may change only through upstream dependency fixes. No repository source code behavior was changed directly.
approved.module_impact: Broad multi-module build graph impact; no single package implementation changed.
approved.cross_module_impact: Aligns security-sensitive transitive dependencies across examples, integrations, storage, model, and server modules.
approved.behavior_impact: Users building affected modules will consume patched dependency versions.
approved.tests_docs: xhigh dependency review reported no findings; final GitHub refresh showed 162 passing checks and no failures/pending checks.
approved.attention_points: Continue watching downstream module consumers for dependency incompatibilities after release tagging.
approved.outcome: xhigh 子审无发现；主线程复核依赖更新范围和最终 checks 后提交 `LGTM`，并按 head `9fb4203fb29787b2011741bf9c8bf54a300fe472` squash merge。
approved.risk: Medium because of broad dependency surface, but acceptable after full green CI.
approved.ci_state: Final refresh showed 162 passing checks, no failing or pending checks before merge.

commented.title: fix: use UPDATE instead of DELETE for soft delete in rotateMemory
commented.author: shanghai-Jerry

inline_comments.body:
This upsert now overwrites any existing row with the same `memory_id`, not just a previously soft-deleted row. If rotating A produces the active id of B, the old insert would fail and roll back, but this path replaces B's content/metadata; keep hard-delete mode as a plain insert and only revive rows proven to be soft-deleted.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里的 upsert 会覆盖任何同 `memory_id` 的现有记录，而不只是复活之前软删除的记录。如果 A 更新后生成的新 id 正好等于仍然 active 的 B，旧逻辑会 duplicate-key 回滚，现在会把 B 的内容和元数据覆盖掉；建议 hard-delete 模式仍保持普通 INSERT，soft-delete 模式也只在确认冲突行已软删除时复活。
</details>

commented.technical_background: `memory/mysqlvec` stores vector memory rows keyed by `memory_id`. `rotateMemory` replaces an entry when updated content hashes to a new ID. In soft-delete mode, the old row is marked deleted instead of removed, and a later insert may need to revive a previously soft-deleted row. That revive path must not overwrite a different still-active memory row.
commented.problem: The PR tries to avoid duplicate-key failure when a soft-deleted old memory ID is reused during A -> B -> A rotation.
commented.problem_framing: The real invariant is stronger than “make duplicate key succeed”: active memories must remain immutable unless they are the row being updated, while soft-deleted duplicates can be revived safely.
commented.root_cause: A blanket MySQL upsert cannot distinguish an intended revive of a deleted row from an accidental collision with an active row.
commented.approach: Changes soft-delete removal from DELETE to UPDATE `deleted_at`, then uses `INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE ... deleted_at = NULL` for the new row.
commented.alternative_designs: Use plain insert for hard-delete mode, and for soft-delete mode revive only if the conflicting row is known to be deleted. Another option is a conditional update with `deleted_at IS NOT NULL` followed by insert/error handling.
commented.tradeoffs: The submitted approach is simple but too broad; the safer design is slightly more SQL but preserves data integrity.
commented.design_assessment: Needs a fix before merge. The current upsert changes collision semantics for active rows and hard-delete mode.
commented.semantic_changes: Would turn active `memory_id` collisions from duplicate-key rollback into overwrite, which is not safe.
commented.module_impact: Affects MySQL vector memory updates and any agent memory using soft delete.
commented.cross_module_impact: Could corrupt memory content visible to agent flows and examples relying on mysqlvec storage.
commented.behavior_impact: Potential active memory row overwrite on ID collision.
commented.tests_docs: Existing tests cover revive-like SQL shape but not active-duplicate preservation.
commented.attention_points: Must restrict revive to soft-deleted rows and keep hard-delete plain insert.
commented.risk: High until fixed because it can overwrite stored memory.
commented.ci_state: Checks were green, but review blocker is data correctness.

commented.title: docs(session): clarify cache-safe summary forking
commented.author: Rememorio

inline_comments.body:
Please make the fork-mode caveat explicit here and mirror it in the Chinese page: `WithPreSummaryHook` text/event edits do not sanitize the cloned parent request. Users who rely on the hook for redaction or filtering can otherwise enable cache-safe forking and still send the original parent messages/tool content to the summary model; they need standalone mode or a sanitized parent request for that use case.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里建议把 fork 模式的边界写得更明确，并同步到中文文档：`WithPreSummaryHook` 对 text/events 的修改不会对克隆出来的父请求做脱敏或过滤。否则依赖 hook 做 redaction/filtering 的用户开启 cache-safe forking 后，仍可能把原始 parent messages / tool content 发给摘要模型；这类场景应使用 standalone 模式，或先构造已脱敏的 parent request。
</details>

commented.modules: session summary docs cache-safe forking section
commented.technical_background: Cache-safe summary forking clones the parent `model.Request` so prompt-cache providers can reuse the same prefix. `WithPreSummaryHook` can rewrite extracted `conversationText` for standalone summary requests, but that rewritten text is not inserted when fork mode clones the existing parent request.
commented.problem: The PR clarifies cache-safe forking prompt rules and fallback behavior.
commented.problem_framing: The dangerous edge is redaction/filtering. Users may assume a pre-summary hook sanitizes all summary inputs, but fork mode sends the cloned parent request.
commented.root_cause: Documentation described that hook text is not embedded in fork mode but did not state the security/privacy implication.
commented.approach: Expands EN/ZH docs with standalone vs fork request construction and prompt rules.
commented.alternative_designs: Docs could recommend disabling fork mode for redaction, or code could allow a sanitized parent request hook. This PR only updates docs.
commented.tradeoffs: Adding the caveat makes docs longer but avoids privacy surprises.
commented.design_assessment: Useful doc improvement, but needs the explicit caveat before merge.
commented.cross_module_impact: Clarifies interaction between summary hook, model request construction, and cache-safe prompt behavior.
commented.behavior_impact: Readers should understand when hook-based redaction is not applied.
commented.tests_docs: Docs-only change; comment requests mirrored caveat in Chinese page too.
commented.attention_points: Add explicit warning that hook text/event edits do not sanitize cloned parent requests.
commented.risk: Medium documentation/privacy risk until caveat is added.
commented.ci_state: Checks were green; blocker is docs accuracy.

commented.title: agent/extension: add goal support
commented.author: bytethm

inline_comments.body:
This guidance still tells the model to call the default `create_goal`/`get_goal`/`update_goal` names even when `WithToolNames` registered custom names. With custom tool names the default tools are absent, so guidance injection should render the configured names, or custom names should disable/reject this guidance path.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里的 guidance 仍然让模型调用默认的 `create_goal` / `get_goal` / `update_goal`，但 `WithToolNames` 可以注册自定义名称，此时默认工具并不存在。建议用配置后的工具名动态渲染 guidance，或在自定义工具名时禁用/拒绝这条默认 guidance 路径。
</details>

Please do not derive the persisted state delta from the rendered tool-result message. `ToolResultMessages` callbacks can replace that message after `Call` has already updated `inv.Session`, so a normal formatter can make `create_goal`/`update_goal` appear to work during this run while emitting no durable state delta for persistent session backends. Read the current goal from the invocation session or attach the delta before result-message rewriting.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里不要从最终渲染出来的 tool-result message 反解持久化 state delta。`ToolResultMessages` callback 可以在 `Call` 已经更新 `inv.Session` 之后替换这条消息，所以普通格式化回调会让 `create_goal` / `update_goal` 在本次运行看起来成功，但对持久化 session 后端不写入 durable state delta。建议从 invocation session 读取当前 goal，或在 result message 被改写前就挂上 delta。
</details>

commented.technical_background: Agent extensions can register tools and model callbacks around an `LLMAgent`. This PR adds session-scoped goal state plus tools (`get_goal`, `create_goal`, `update_goal`) and an AfterModel hook that blocks premature final responses by returning a non-final control response. Durable goal state must survive persistent session backends through event state deltas, not only in-memory mutation during a single invocation.
commented.problem: The PR introduces a Goal extension so an agent can keep working until a session goal is complete or blocked.
commented.problem_framing: The root problem is lifecycle ownership: a model final response is not enough when an explicit persistent goal remains active. The extension is a reasonable architecture, but its tool naming and persistence hooks must align with existing extension customization and callback behavior.
commented.root_cause: Two integration seams were missed: custom tool names are public options, and framework tool-result callbacks can rewrite the message used for state delta extraction.
commented.approach: Adds goal package, options, state helpers, tools, enforcement callbacks, tests, docs, and example CLI.
commented.alternative_designs: Goal state could be a runner-level mode or external orchestration layer. An LLMAgent extension is lighter, but must respect tool callback and session persistence contracts.
commented.tradeoffs: The extension design is promising and contained, but correctness depends on precise model guidance and durable state delta generation.
commented.design_assessment: Needs fixes before merge. The architecture is acceptable, but custom tool names and persistent backends are broken in realistic configurations.
commented.change_inventory: New `agent/extension/goal` package, docs nav/pages, `examples/goal`, and tests.
commented.api_surface: Adds exported `goal.New`, options (`WithToolNames`, `WithMaxRetries`, etc.), `Goal`, `Start`, default constants, and tool names.
commented.semantic_changes: Active goals can cause final responses to be converted into non-final control events until the model updates goal state or retry budget is exhausted.
commented.module_impact: Affects LLMAgent extension users and session backends.
commented.cross_module_impact: Interacts with model callbacks, tool callbacks, session state delta, runner completion, docs, and examples.
commented.behavior_impact: Could keep a run alive while a goal is active. Current issues could make the model call missing custom tools or lose goal state on persistent backends.
commented.tests_docs: Good baseline tests, but missing custom-tool guidance and tool-result callback state-delta regression tests.
commented.attention_points: Fix guidance rendering for custom names and derive state delta from invocation session instead of rendered tool-result content.
commented.risk: High for custom configurations and persistent sessions until fixed.
commented.ci_state: Checks were green; blockers are API/persistence correctness.

commented.title: session/summary: add trigger reports
commented.author: Rememorio

inline_comments.body:
Forced full-session cascade reports still lose the original branch key here. The cascade call targets the all-contents summary with `filterKey == ""`, while the branch that triggered it is only carried in `summaryTriggerFilterKeyFromContext(ctx)`, so hooks still see an empty `Trigger.FilterKey`; prefer that context trigger key for forced all-contents reports and add a cascade regression test.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里的 forced full-session cascade report 仍然会丢掉原始分支 key。cascade 调用生成 all-contents summary 时 `filterKey == ""`，真正触发它的分支只在 `summaryTriggerFilterKeyFromContext(ctx)` 里，所以 hook 仍会看到空的 `Trigger.FilterKey`；建议 forced all-contents report 优先使用 context 中的 trigger key，并补一个 cascade 回归测试。
</details>

This records a model-call report even when `BeforeModel` returned a `CustomResponse` and no summary model request is sent. That overcounts callback-served summaries with a normal `Call.Mode` and prompt-token estimate; either record only before `GenerateContent`, or emit an explicit callback/custom call mode and test the custom-response path.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里在 `BeforeModel` 返回 `CustomResponse`、实际不会调用摘要模型时也记录了一次 model-call report。这样 callback 直接返回摘要的场景会被记成普通 `Call.Mode` 和 prompt-token 估算，造成报告过计；建议只在真正调用 `GenerateContent` 前记录，或显式记录 callback/custom 模式，并补上 custom-response 路径测试。
</details>

commented.modules: session/internal/summary and session/summary trigger report telemetry
commented.technical_background: Session summaries can be generated for filtered branches and for the all-contents summary. Trigger reports let hooks/observers know why a summary fired and which filter key caused it. In cascade flows, a branch summary can force a full-session summary; attribution has to preserve the original branch key even when the target summary key is all-contents.
commented.problem: The PR adds trigger reports and summary-call reporting so hooks can observe why summaries were generated.
commented.problem_framing: The root need is observability for summary generation. The tricky part is that reporting must distinguish the target summary being written from the trigger that caused the call.
commented.root_cause: The first fix wrote `filterKey` from the target call into reports, but cascade all-contents calls have an empty/all filter and carry the original branch trigger only through context.
commented.approach: Adds report structs/tests and records trigger/call/usage data around summary checks and model calls.
commented.alternative_designs: Report generation could use an explicit trigger object passed through the whole call graph instead of context fallback. That would be more explicit but more invasive.
commented.tradeoffs: The current design is mostly lightweight, but context-carried trigger state needs careful precedence in forced cascade paths.
commented.design_assessment: Needs fixes before merge. The observability surface is valuable, but inaccurate reports are worse than missing reports for hooks that make decisions from them.
commented.change_inventory: Adds summary report fields, trigger report tests, call/usage reporting, and docs.
commented.api_surface: Adds/extends report-facing summary types and hook-visible report data.
commented.semantic_changes: Summary generation can now expose trigger/call metadata to hooks; no intended change to generated summary text.
commented.module_impact: Affects summary checker/summarizer observability and hook users.
commented.cross_module_impact: Interacts with `session/internal/summary`, `session/summary`, model callbacks, and report hooks.
commented.behavior_impact: Hooks can attribute summary calls, but current cascade and callback cases can misreport.
commented.tests_docs: Existing tests cover ordinary forced reports; missing cascade trigger-key and BeforeModel custom-response report tests.
commented.attention_points: Fix cascade trigger key and avoid recording model-call reports for callback-served summaries.
commented.risk: Medium; observability correctness bug could mislead summary hooks.
commented.ci_state: Checks were green; blockers are report correctness.

commented.title: session/mysql: skip schema DDL for pre-existing tables on init
commented.author: hyprh

inline_comments.body:
The new fatal UNIQUE-index check still only compares index names and columns; it never reads `information_schema.statistics.NON_UNIQUE`. A pre-existing non-unique index with the expected name and columns will pass here, so the skip-DDL path can still start without the uniqueness constraint; please store `NON_UNIQUE` per index and fail when `expected.unique && NON_UNIQUE != 0`.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
新的 fatal UNIQUE-index 校验仍然只比较索引名和列，没有读取 `information_schema.statistics.NON_UNIQUE`。如果已有索引名字和列都正确但其实不是唯一索引，这里会通过，skip-DDL 路径仍可能在没有唯一约束的情况下启动；建议按索引保存 `NON_UNIQUE`，并在 `expected.unique && NON_UNIQUE != 0` 时失败。
</details>

commented.modules: session/mysql schema bootstrap and index verification
commented.technical_background: MySQL session storage uses active-row uniqueness constraints to make app/user/session/summary state writes safe. This PR stops running DDL against existing tables, which is operationally safer, but it makes verification the only guard that prevents startup with a drifted schema.
commented.problem: The PR makes MySQL session init skip schema DDL for pre-existing tables and fail startup when required unique indexes are missing or invalid.
commented.problem_framing: The root problem is balancing operator-managed schemas with correctness-critical uniqueness constraints. Once init stops fixing existing tables, verification must be strong enough to catch every uniqueness drift.
commented.root_cause: The new verification checks index name and columns but not whether an index is actually unique.
commented.approach: Groups indexes by table for first-time creation, skips DDL for existing tables, verifies columns/indexes, and makes missing/wrong unique indexes fatal.
commented.alternative_designs: Could always retry missing indexes, but that risks live DDL. The chosen skip-and-verify design is safer if verification is complete.
commented.tradeoffs: Avoiding live DDL is operationally conservative, but it raises the bar for schema validation.
commented.design_assessment: Direction is good but incomplete. It must read `NON_UNIQUE` to prove same-name same-column indexes enforce uniqueness.
commented.change_inventory: Changed `initDB`, added `createTableWithIndexes`, changed `verifyIndexes`, and added tests for existing tables and missing unique indexes.
commented.api_surface: No exported Go API change; startup behavior changes for drifted MySQL session schemas.
commented.semantic_changes: Existing tables no longer receive DDL automatically. Missing/incorrect unique index should fail startup, but non-unique same-name index currently slips through.
commented.cross_module_impact: Impacts runner/session users backed by MySQL and operators managing session schema manually.
commented.behavior_impact: Could still start without true uniqueness if a non-unique index has the expected name/columns.
commented.tests_docs: Needs a regression test for same-name/same-columns-but-non-unique.
commented.risk: High for schema drift correctness until fixed.
commented.ci_state: Checks were green; blocker is schema validation correctness.

commented.title: session/postgres: surface init failures as errors and fail on missing unique index
commented.author: hyprh

inline_comments.body:
Checking only the index name and `indisunique` is still not enough for the required partial unique index. A same-name unique index on the wrong columns, or without the `deleted_at IS NULL` predicate, passes this map but does not satisfy the `ON CONFLICT ... WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` contract; please verify the indexed columns and predicate via `pg_index`/`pg_attribute`/`pg_get_expr` or `pg_get_indexdef`.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
只检查索引名和 `indisunique` 仍然不足以确认这是需要的 partial unique index。如果已有同名唯一索引列不对，或缺少 `deleted_at IS NULL` predicate，这里仍会通过，但它并不满足 `ON CONFLICT ... WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` 的约束目标；建议通过 `pg_index` / `pg_attribute` / `pg_get_expr` 或 `pg_get_indexdef` 同时校验索引列和 predicate。
</details>

commented.modules: session/postgres schema bootstrap and index verification
commented.technical_background: Postgres session storage uses partial unique indexes such as `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` to enforce one active row while preserving soft-deleted history. `ON CONFLICT ... WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` depends on a matching unique index; a merely same-name unique index is not enough if columns or predicate differ.
commented.problem: The PR changes Postgres init failures from panics to returned errors and makes missing/non-unique required indexes fatal.
commented.problem_framing: The root problem is startup safety: the service should not start when storage invariants are absent, but the verifier must validate the actual invariant, not just index names.
commented.root_cause: The new verifier reads `pg_index.indisunique` but not indexed columns or the partial predicate.
commented.approach: Returns errors from `initDB`, closes client pool on init failure, queries indexes from pg catalogs, and fails on missing or non-unique `unique_active` indexes.
commented.alternative_designs: Could re-run DDL, but operator-managed schemas may forbid it. Verification through pg catalog metadata is the safer path.
commented.tradeoffs: Catalog verification is more complex but necessary for correctness when DDL is not automatically trusted.
commented.design_assessment: Needs fix before merge. The resource cleanup direction is good, but the index verifier is still too weak for partial unique indexes.
commented.change_inventory: Modified `initDB`, `verifySchema`, `verifyIndexes`, service constructor cleanup, and schema tests.
commented.api_surface: No exported API change; `NewService` startup error behavior changes from panic/crash to returned error.
commented.semantic_changes: Startup can now return schema errors; missing/non-unique indexes are intended to be fatal. Current verifier can still accept wrong columns/predicate.
commented.module_impact: Affects Postgres session service initialization and operators with pre-provisioned schemas.
commented.cross_module_impact: Impacts any runner/session user backed by Postgres.
commented.behavior_impact: Could still start with a unique index that does not match `ON CONFLICT ... WHERE deleted_at IS NULL`.
commented.tests_docs: Needs tests for same-name unique index on wrong columns and missing partial predicate.
commented.risk: High for schema drift correctness until fixed. `codecov/patch` was also non-green, so merge would remain blocked even after approval.
commented.ci_state: Only `codecov/patch` was non-green initially; a correctness comment was posted instead of approval.

commented.title: openclaw: keep workflow guidance out of memory
commented.author: Rememorio

inline_comments.body:
Refreshing only through `EnsureMemory`/`UpdateMemory` misses the secondary-memory context path that resolves `MemoryPath` and calls `ReadFile` with `ensure=false`. Existing files that still contain the immediately previous default template can therefore be read as user memory before `IsDefaultTemplate` is checked; add a refresh-on-read path or a refresh-existing helper used by the context builder.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
只在 `EnsureMemory` / `UpdateMemory` 里刷新旧模板，会漏掉 secondary-memory context 那条通过 `MemoryPath` 后直接 `ReadFile`、且 `ensure=false` 的路径。已有文件如果仍是上一版默认模板，就可能在 `IsDefaultTemplate` 判断前被当成用户记忆读入；建议补一个 read 时刷新，或让 context builder 调用 refresh-existing helper。
</details>

Please keep the ban on secrets and large transcripts absolute. As written, `secrets` and `large transcripts` are in the same `unless the user explicitly asks` clause as workflows/formats, so the prompt can allow saving them to memory when the user asks; split this into an unconditional `Do not store secrets or large transcripts` sentence, then apply the opt-in exception only to reusable workflows/output formats/tool procedures/post-task feedback.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
建议继续把 secrets 和 large transcripts 的禁令保持为绝对规则。现在它们和 workflows/formats 放在同一个 `unless the user explicitly asks` 从句里，模型会被允许在用户明确要求时把 secrets 或大段 transcript 存进 memory；建议拆成一句无条件的 `Do not store secrets or large transcripts`，再把显式 opt-in 例外只用于 reusable workflows、output formats、tool procedures 和 post-task feedback。
</details>

commented.modules: openclaw memory file templates, app prompts, memory context path
commented.technical_background: OpenClaw exposes visible `MEMORY.md` files. The default template should not be treated as user memory. This PR tries to migrate old visible template wording away from reusable workflow storage and toward user preferences, while preserving real user bullets. Context-building paths must refresh or ignore managed templates before injecting memory text into the agent prompt.
commented.problem: The PR tries to keep workflow guidance out of memory by refreshing older default templates and tightening instructions around what belongs in memory.
commented.problem_framing: The deeper issue is separating durable user memory from reusable workflows, prompts, and secrets. Both code paths and prompt wording must preserve that boundary.
commented.root_cause: Template refresh was added only to ensure/update paths, not read-only context paths; prompt wording also grouped secrets/transcripts under an opt-in exception.
commented.approach: Adds template refresh helpers, updates default template text, refreshes existing files during `EnsureMemory`/`UpdateMemory`, and changes app-level memory instructions.
commented.alternative_designs: A more complete design would centralize every memory-file read through a store method that refreshes managed templates and keeps sensitive-content policy separate from opt-in workflow storage.
commented.tradeoffs: Refreshing on write is cheap but misses read-only injection paths. Central read refresh is slightly more invasive but protects all prompt construction.
commented.design_assessment: Needs fixes before merge. The intent is good, but two important boundary leaks remain.
commented.change_inventory: Changed `openclaw/app/app.go`, memory store/template helpers and tests, and octool memory wording.
commented.api_surface: No exported Go API change; prompt/memory file behavior changes.
commented.semantic_changes: Existing managed template text can be refreshed during ensure/update, but direct read paths still bypass it.
commented.module_impact: Affects OpenClaw memory-file context and agent instructions.
commented.cross_module_impact: Touches memory store, app prompt construction, and octool memory guidance.
commented.behavior_impact: Stale template text can still be injected as memory in `ensure=false` secondary context paths; secrets/transcripts policy is weakened by wording.
commented.tests_docs: Needs `ensure=false` read-path regression and prompt-policy split.
commented.attention_points: Keep secrets/large transcripts absolute no-store rules.
commented.risk: High for prompt safety/memory hygiene until fixed.
commented.ci_state: Checks were green; blockers are memory/prompt policy correctness.

follow_up: [#2029](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2029) 修复 cascade FilterKey 和 BeforeModel CustomResponse 报告后可优先复审。
[#2022](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2022) / [#2026](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2026) 都是“已有表跳过 DDL”后的唯一索引验证强度问题，建议成对关注。
[#2018](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2018) 涉及 OpenClaw memory 安全边界，修复后应补 ensure=false 读取路径测试。
[#1980](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/1980) 是较大的新 extension，修复 custom tool names 与持久化 state delta 后需要重新跑 xhigh。
[#1996](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/1996) 需要真正覆盖 streaming `accumulateChunk` 路径后再继续。
[#1400](https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/1400) 仍 locked 且有公开接口破坏性变更 blocker；unlock 后也要先解决接口兼容。
skill_evolution: 本轮新增 backlog：旧 run_state 虽非空但缺新 fingerprint 字段，planner 全量 heavy；需要 schema migration/backfill。
bulk GraphQL 继续因 504 不稳定，repo-managed bounded collector 仍是最高优先级自动化债务。

blockers.summary: Sandbox exec_command path bypasses host command safety policy/output redactor.
Rejects existing type none/local configs instead of keeping legacy aliases.

Please do not treat every `GetSession` error as a missing session. Session services can return real backend/read errors here, and this code currently falls through to `CreateSession`, which can create or overwrite goal state instead of surfacing the persistence failure. Return non-nil `getErr`; only call `CreateSession` when `GetSession` succeeds with a nil session, and add a fake-service regression test for that path.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里把所有 `GetSession` 错误都当成 session 不存在处理了。session service 可能在这里返回真实的后端/读取错误；当前代码会继续走 `CreateSession`，可能在持久化失败时创建或覆盖 goal state。请在 `getErr != nil` 时直接返回错误，只在 `GetSession` 成功但 `sess == nil` 时创建 session，并补一个 fake service 回归测试。
</details>

1996.title: fix: DeepSeek cached_tokens always 0 due to missing prompt_cache_hit_…
1996.author: shanghai-Jerry

blockers.summary:
Please add a streaming-path regression test here. The new tests cover the helper and non-streaming conversion, but this accumulator path is the one that feeds streamed DeepSeek usage into the final response; without a chunk-level test carrying `usage.prompt_cache_hit_tokens` through `accumulateChunk` or `applyOpenAISDKTokenDetailsAccumulationFix`, the streaming behavior is still not verified.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里是流式路径的新增接入点。当前 PR 覆盖了 helper 和非流式 `completionUsageToModelUsage`，但没有真实经过 streaming accumulator 的回归测试；如果以后这里的 chunk accumulation 退化，现有测试发现不了。请补一条带 `usage.prompt_cache_hit_tokens` 的 streaming/chunk 级测试。
</details>

This overstates both guarantees: `EnqueueSummaryJob` can still fall back to synchronous summary creation when the worker or queue is unavailable, and the session/filterKey serialization is process-local rather than a cross-instance guarantee. Please qualify the non-blocking and duplicate-prevention wording here and mirror the same caveat in the zh doc.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里把两个保证说得过强了：当 async worker 或队列不可用时，`EnqueueSummaryJob` 仍可能 fallback 到同步摘要创建；而 session/filterKey 串行化也是进程内保证，不是跨实例保证。请把“不会阻塞输出”和“不会重复昂贵摘要”的表述改成带条件的说明，并同步中文文档。
</details>

Please bump the OTel SDK modules to `v1.41.0` wherever this PR moves the surrounding `go.opentelemetry.io/otel`, `otel/metric`, or `otel/trace` modules to `v1.41.0`. This file still leaves `otel/sdk` on `v1.40.0`, and the same mismatch remains in `examples/go.mod`, `examples/google/go.mod`, `examples/knowledge/go.mod`, `examples/provider/go.mod`, `examples/tailor/go.mod`, `model/provider/go.mod`, `openclaw/go.mod`, `server/evaluation/go.mod`, and `server/promptiter/go.mod`. `runner/bestofn` already fixed the same drift by moving SDK to `v1.41.0`; please apply that consistently. Also align `session/clickhouse/go.mod` by moving `otel/sdk` from `v1.34.0` to the `v1.35.0` release train used by its OTel API modules.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这些模块本次把 OTel API/metric/trace 升到了 `v1.41.0`，但 SDK 仍停在 `v1.40.0`；`runner/bestofn` 已经按同类问题修到 `v1.41.0`，这里也需要同步，否则依赖更新后仍残留 OpenTelemetry 版本族不一致。另一个同类点是 `session/clickhouse/go.mod`：API/metric/trace 是 `v1.35.0`，SDK 仍是 `v1.34.0`，也需要对齐。
</details>

2022.title: session/mysql: skip schema DDL for pre-existing tables on init
2022.author: hyprh

blockers.summary:
Please do not treat every existing table as operator-provisioned. If a previous bootstrap created the table and then failed while creating a UNIQUE index, the next startup takes this skip path, `verifySchema` only logs the missing index, and the service can run without the uniqueness constraints that session/state writes rely on. Please track/retry incomplete bootstrap tables or make missing UNIQUE indexes fatal for tables created by init.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里把“运维预置的完整表”和“上次 init 建表成功但建唯一索引失败留下的半成品表”混在一起了。后者下次启动会跳过索引 DDL，而唯一索引缺失只打 ERROR 日志不阻止启动，可能让 session/app/user state 在缺少唯一约束时继续写入。请让 init 能识别并重试/阻断这种未完成的 bootstrap 状态，或者对 init 创建过的表缺少 UNIQUE index 直接失败。
</details>
